behavior_summary: This script implements an NPC ambient/interaction/alert behavior set for a peasant-like actor. It manages: cleanup when the player leaves the NPC's fast-area trigger; a short 'U8P_PESANT2::look' description; player-initiated interaction ('U8P_PESANT2::use') that begins a conversation and plays short greeting barks; ambient, map-dependent periodic barks scheduled by timers; and a reaction to being targeted/attacked that can notify guards and schedule followups.
Timers/delays are implemented by spawning a shared helper (FREE::func1C19) and references/ timers are tracked via FREE::resetRef so they can be cancelled on leave or before new interactions.

Ambient speech is gated by player distance and an engine-level 'somebodyTalking' state to avoid overlapping lines. Map 0x29 triggers a separate ambient bark set (U8P_PESANT2::func0468) while other maps U8P_PESANT2::use U8P_PESANT2::func06CD.

The U8P_PESANT2::cast(uword) process reacts to targeting: if the player is the attacker it calls the guard system (CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime), clears combat state, and emits panic barks; otherwise it retries with a short countdown using spawn delays.
notable_control_flow: U8P_PESANT2::leaveFastArea — unconditional cleanup: calls FREE::resetRef on several reference ids to cancel conversation/timer state whenever an actor leaves the fast-area.
U8P_PESANT2::enterFastArea — early checks (an ID/threshold test, then Npc::isDead) and a map dispatch: if Npc::getMap(this) == 0x29 then call U8P_PESANT2::func0468 else call U8P_PESANT2::func06CD.

U8P_PESANT2::func0468 and U8P_PESANT2::func06CD — schedule respawned timers (spawn -> FREE::func1C19), choose a random bark index, check FREE::getDistanceBetween(0x01,this) and a 'somebodyTalking' guard, then call METHOD::func15E7 to emit a bark.

U8P_PESANT2::use — clears/cancels relevant refs, picks a greeting at random, calls METHOD::beginConversationNoMap and METHOD::func15E7 to run short player-facing lines, and uses a spawned delay to manage conversation timing.

U8P_PESANT2::cast(uword) — initializes a retry counter, if no target repeatedly spawns a short delay and decrements the counter; if the target is the player it calls CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime, spawns a longer delay, clears in-combat state (Npc::clrInCombat) and emits an alarm bark chosen randomly.
open_questions: What precisely does the numeric check 'this > 0x00FA' represent? (appears to be an ID/threshold gate — confidence: medium).
What is the semantic meaning of the uword parameter to U8P_PESANT2::cast(uword)? (likely a spell/target id or damage source indicator — confidence: low).
Is 'somebodyTalking' an explicit global flag in script space or purely engine state provided at runtime? (appears engine-level read-only — confidence: medium).
Exact schedule semantics and units for spawn -> FREE::func1C19 delays (milliseconds vs ticks) are not encoded here — inferred behavior only (confidence: low).

main_function.name: U8P_PESANT2::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Unconditionally cancel and clean up area-specific timers, conversation references, and other ephemeral state for this NPC when an actor leaves its fast-area trigger so ambient/interaction behaviors stop.
entry_conditions: Called by the engine when an actor (player or other relevant entity) exits the NPC's fast-area trigger region.
No parameters; executes cleanup regardless of NPC state.

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::look
function_map.role: player 'U8P_PESANT2::look' handler; emits a short description or dead variant

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::enterFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area entry handler; checks and dispatches to ambient bark schedulers

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::cast
function_map.role: reaction to being U8P_PESANT2::cast on / targeted: retries or notifies guard system and schedules followups

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::use
function_map.role: player interaction handler; begins conversation, plays greeting barks and schedules conversation timers

function_map.name: U8P_PESANT2::func06CD
function_map.role: ambient/drunken bark scheduler/emitter for non-0x29 maps

internal_calls.purpose: Dispatch to map-specific ambient scheduler when Npc::getMap(this) == 0x29
Dispatch to alternate ambient scheduler for maps other than 0x29

external_calls.purpose: Clear stored references/timers/state ids (refs 0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200) to cancel ongoing behaviors
Spawn a delayed/timed helper process to schedule next ambient check or bark
Spawn a delayed/timed helper process to schedule next ambient check or bark
Measure distance from player (entity 0x01) to this NPC to gate ambient barks
Measure distance from player to NPC to gate ambient barks
Emit or queue the chosen ambient bark/dialogue line
Emit or queue the chosen ambient/drunken bark/dialogue line
Begin a conversation with the player without changing map context
Play greeting/interaction lines during the conversation flow
Notify guard/crime system when the player is detected as the attacker/target
Clear NPC's in-combat state after scheduling guard response/delay
Spawn short or longer delays used for retry or post-attack scheduling
Decide whether to emit a 'dead' description or the live description
Short-circuit ambient scheduling for dead NPCs

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements an NPC ambient/interaction/alert behavior set for a peasant-like actor. It manages: cleanup when the player leaves the NPC's fast-area trigger; a short 'U8P_PESANT2::look' description; player-initiated interaction ('U8P_PESANT2::use') that begins a conversation and plays short greeting barks; ambient, map-dependent periodic barks scheduled by timers; and a reaction to being targeted/attacked that can notify guards and schedule followups.
